prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "俯卧 I 字" (prone I raise) exercise, view from above and slightly behind. Person posture: Athletic person doing a prone I (Y-T-W-I series): lying face-down on the floor on a mat, legs extended together; both arms fully extended overhead in a straight line with the body (forming the letter I from above), thumbs pointing toward the ceiling, palms facing each other; arms lifted off the ground a few inches by squeezing the upper back; chin slightly tucked, forehead just off the ground; this works the lower trapezius and shoulder stabilizers; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: lower trapezius and rhomboids (mid-back retraction). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: rear deltoid and erector spinae. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "下斜方肌" pointing to the lower trapezius; "菱形肌" pointing to the rhomboids; "三角肌后束 / 竖脊肌" pointing to the rear deltoid and erector spinae. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
